     4→//! VOK delegates unknown subcommands here via external_subcommand pattern.
     5→//!
     6→//! All commands are handled entirely here, including notch/chalk which
     7→//! use the vvc crate for commit operations.
     8→

   317→// ============================================================================
   318→// Public dispatch API
   319→// ============================================================================
   320→
   321→/// Dispatch JJK commands from raw arguments.
   322→///
   323→/// Called by VOK when it receives an external subcommand starting with "jjx_".
   324→/// The first element of `args` should be the subcommand name (e.g., "jjx_muster").
   325→///
   326→/// Returns exit code (0 for success, non-zero for failure).
   327→pub fn jjrx_dispatch(args: &[OsString]) -> i32 {
   328→    // Prepend synthetic binary name for clap parsing.
   329→    // Clap expects args[0] to be the binary name (used in help text).
   330→    // VOK passes ["jjx_muster", ...] so we prepend "jjx" to get ["jjx", "jjx_muster", ...].
   331→    let mut full_args = vec![OsString::from("jjx")];
   332→    full_args.extend(args.iter().cloned());
   333→
   334→    // Parse the subcommand and arguments
   335→    let parsed = match jjrx_JjxCommands::try_parse_from(&full_args) {
   336→        Ok(cmd) => cmd,
   337→        Err(e) => {
   338→            // Let clap handle help/version/error display
   339→            e.print().ok();
   340→            return if e.kind() == clap::error::ErrorKind::DisplayHelp
   341→                   || e.kind() == clap::error::ErrorKind::DisplayVersion {
   342→                0
   343→            } else {
   344→                1
   345→            };
   346→        }
   347→    };
   348→
   349→    match parsed {
   350→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Notch(args) => zjjrx_run_notch(args),
   351→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Chalk(args) => zjjrx_run_chalk(args),
   352→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Rein(args) => zjjrx_run_rein(args),
   353→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Validate(args) => zjjrx_run_validate(args),
   354→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Muster(args) => zjjrx_run_muster(args),
   355→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Saddle(args) => zjjrx_run_saddle(args),
   356→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Parade(args) => zjjrx_run_parade(args),
   357→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Retire(args) => zjjrx_run_retire(args),
   358→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Nominate(args) => zjjrx_run_nominate(args),
   359→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Slate(args) => zjjrx_run_slate(args),
   360→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Rail(args) => zjjrx_run_rail(args),
   361→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Tally(args) => zjjrx_run_tally(args),
   362→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Draft(args) => zjjrx_run_draft(args),
   363→    }
   364→}
   365→
   366→/// Check if a command name is a JJK command
   367→pub fn jjrx_is_jjk_command(name: &str) -> bool {
   368→    name.starts_with("jjx_")
   369→}

   371→// ============================================================================
   372→// Notch/Chalk implementations (use vvc for commits)
   373→// ============================================================================
   374→
   375→fn zjjrx_run_notch(args: jjrx_NotchArgs) -> i32 {
   376→    let coronet = match Coronet::jjrf_parse(&args.coronet) {
   377→        Ok(c) => c,
   378→        Err(e) => {
   379→            eprintln!("jjx_notch: error: {}", e);
   380→            return 1;
   381→        }
   382→    };
   383→
   384→    let prefix = format_notch_prefix(&coronet);
   385→
   386→    let commit_args = vvc::vvcc_CommitArgs {
   387→        prefix: Some(prefix),
   388→        message: None,
   389→        allow_empty: false,
   390→        no_stage: false,
   391→        size_limit: args.size_limit,
   392→        warn_limit: None,
   393→    };
   394→
   395→    vvc::commit(&commit_args)
   396→}
   397→
   398→fn zjjrx_run_chalk(args: jjrx_ChalkArgs) -> i32 {
   399→    let marker = match ChalkMarker::jjrn_parse(&args.marker) {
   400→        Ok(m) => m,
   401→        Err(e) => {
   402→            eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: {}", e);
   403→            return 1;
   404→        }
   405→    };
   406→
   407→    // Try parsing as Coronet first (5 base64 chars), then as Firemark (2 base64 chars)
   408→    let identity = args.identity.strip_prefix('₢').or_else(|| args.identity.strip_prefix('₣')).unwrap_or(&args.identity);
   409→
   410→    let message = if identity.len() == 5 {
   411→        // Coronet - pace-level chalk
   412→        let coronet = match Coronet::jjrf_parse(&args.identity) {
   413→            Ok(c) => c,
   414→            Err(e) => {
   415→                eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: {}", e);
   416→                return 1;
   417→            }
   418→        };
   419→        format_chalk_message(&coronet, marker, &args.description)
   420→    } else if identity.len() == 2 {
   421→        // Firemark - heat-level (discussion only)
   422→        if marker.jjrn_requires_pace() {
   423→            eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: {} marker requires a Coronet (pace identity), not a Firemark", marker.jjrn_as_str());
   424→            return 1;
   425→        }
   426→        let firemark = match Firemark::jjrf_parse(&args.identity) {
   427→            Ok(fm) => fm,
   428→            Err(e) => {
   429→                eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: {}", e);
   430→                return 1;
   431→            }
   432→        };
   433→        format_heat_discussion(&firemark, &args.description)
   434→    } else {
   435→        eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: identity must be Coronet (5 chars) or Firemark (2 chars), got {} chars", identity.len());
   436→        return 1;
   437→    };
   438→
   439→    let commit_args = vvc::vvcc_CommitArgs {
   440→        prefix: None,
   441→        message: Some(message),
   442→        allow_empty: true,
   443→        no_stage: true,
   444→        size_limit: None,
   445→        warn_limit: None,
   446→    };
   447→
   448→    vvc::commit(&commit_args)
   449→}

   455→fn zjjrx_run_rein(args: zjjrx_ReinArgs) -> i32 {

   459→        firemark: args.firemark,
   460→        limit: args.limit,
   461→    };
   462→
   463→    run(rein_args)
   464→}

   466→fn zjjrx_run_validate(args: zjjrx_ValidateArgs) -> i32 {
   467→    let gallops = match Gallops::jjrg_load(&args.file) {
   468→        Ok(g) => g,
   469→        Err(e) => {
   470→            eprintln!("jjx_validate: error loading Gallops: {}", e);
   471→            return 1;
   472→        }
   473→    };
   474→
   475→    match gallops.jjrg_validate() {
   476→        Ok(()) => {
   477→            println!("Gallops validation passed");
   478→            0
   479→        }
   480→        Err(errors) => {
   481→            eprintln!("jjx_validate: validation failed with {} error(s):", errors.len());
   482→            for error in errors {
   483→                eprintln!("  - {}", error);
   484→            }
   485→            1
   486→        }
   487→    }
   488→}
   489→
   490→fn zjjrx_run_muster(args: zjjrx_MusterArgs) -> i32 {
   491→    use crate::jjrg_gallops::jjrg_HeatStatus as HeatStatus;
   492→    use crate::jjrq_query::{jjrq_MusterArgs as LibMusterArgs, jjrq_run_muster as lib_run_muster};
   493→
   494→    let status = match &args.status {
   495→        Some(s) => match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {
   496→            "current" => Some(HeatStatus::Current),
   497→            "retired" => Some(HeatStatus::Retired),
   498→            _ => {
   499→                eprintln!("jjx_muster: error: invalid status '{}', must be 'current' or 'retired'", s);
   500→                return 1;
   501→            }
   502→        },
   503→        None => None,
   504→    };
   505→
   506→    let muster_args = LibMusterArgs {
   507→        file: args.file,
   508→        status,
   509→    };
   510→
   511→    lib_run_muster(muster_args)
   512→}

   533→fn zjjrx_run_parade(args: zjjrx_ParadeArgs) -> i32 {
   534→    use crate::jjrq_query::{jjrq_ParadeArgs as LibParadeArgs, jjrq_ParadeFormat as ParadeFormat, jjrq_run_parade as lib_run_parade};
   535→
   536→    let firemark = match Firemark::jjrf_parse(&args.firemark) {
   537→        Ok(fm) => fm,
   538→        Err(e) => {
   539→            eprintln!("jjx_parade: error: {}", e);
   540→            return 1;
   541→        }
   542→    };
   543→
   544→    let format = match args.format {
   545→        zjjrx_ParadeFormatArg::Overview => ParadeFormat::Overview,
   546→        zjjrx_ParadeFormatArg::Order => ParadeFormat::Order,
   547→        zjjrx_ParadeFormatArg::Detail => ParadeFormat::Detail,

   561→fn zjjrx_run_retire(args: zjjrx_RetireArgs) -> i32 {
   562→    use crate::jjrc_core::jjrc_timestamp_date;
   563→    use crate::jjrg_gallops::jjrg_RetireArgs as LibRetireArgs;
   564→    use crate::jjrs_steeplechase::{jjrs_ReinArgs as ReinArgs, jjrs_get_entries as get_entries};
   565→    use std::path::Path;
   566→
   567→    let firemark = match Firemark::jjrf_parse(&args.firemark) {

   321→// ============================================================================
   322→// Public dispatch API
   323→// ============================================================================
   324→
   325→/// Dispatch JJK commands from raw arguments.
   326→///
   327→/// Called by VOK when it receives an external subcommand starting with "jjx_".
   328→/// The first element of `args` should be the subcommand name (e.g., "jjx_muster").
   329→///
   330→/// Returns exit code (0 for success, non-zero for failure).
   331→pub fn jjrx_dispatch(args: &[OsString]) -> i32 {
   332→    // Prepend synthetic binary name for clap parsing.
   333→    // Clap expects args[0] to be the binary name (used in help text).
   334→    // VOK passes ["jjx_muster", ...] so we prepend "jjx" to get ["jjx", "jjx_muster", ...].
   335→    let mut full_args = vec![OsString::from("jjx")];
   336→    full_args.extend(args.iter().cloned());
   337→
   338→    // Parse the subcommand and arguments
   339→    let parsed = match jjrx_JjxCommands::try_parse_from(&full_args) {
   340→        Ok(cmd) => cmd,
   341→        Err(e) => {
   342→            // Let clap handle help/version/error display
   343→            e.print().ok();
   344→            return if e.kind() == clap::error::ErrorKind::DisplayHelp
   345→                   || e.kind() == clap::error::ErrorKind::DisplayVersion {
   346→                0
   347→            } else {
   348→                1
   349→            };
   350→        }
   351→    };
   352→
   353→    match parsed {
   354→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Notch(args) => zjjrx_run_notch(args),
   355→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Chalk(args) => zjjrx_run_chalk(args),
   356→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Rein(args) => zjjrx_run_rein(args),
   357→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Validate(args) => zjjrx_run_validate(args),
   358→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Muster(args) => zjjrx_run_muster(args),
   359→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Saddle(args) => zjjrx_run_saddle(args),
   360→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Parade(args) => zjjrx_run_parade(args),
   361→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Retire(args) => zjjrx_run_retire(args),
   362→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Nominate(args) => zjjrx_run_nominate(args),
   363→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Slate(args) => zjjrx_run_slate(args),
   364→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Rail(args) => zjjrx_run_rail(args),
   365→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Tally(args) => zjjrx_run_tally(args),
   366→        jjrx_JjxCommands::Draft(args) => zjjrx_run_draft(args),
   367→    }
   368→}
   369→
   370→/// Check if a command name is a JJK command
   371→pub fn jjrx_is_jjk_command(name: &str) -> bool {
   372→    name.starts_with("jjx_")
   373→}

   379→fn zjjrx_run_notch(args: jjrx_NotchArgs) -> i32 {
   380→    let coronet = match Coronet::jjrf_parse(&args.coronet) {
   381→        Ok(c) => c,
   382→        Err(e) => {
   383→            eprintln!("jjx_notch: error: {}", e);
   384→            return 1;
   385→        }
   386→    };
   387→
   388→    let prefix = format_notch_prefix(&coronet);
   389→
   390→    let commit_args = vvc::vvcc_CommitArgs {
   391→        prefix: Some(prefix),
   392→        message: None,
   393→        allow_empty: false,
   394→        no_stage: false,
   395→        size_limit: args.size_limit,
   396→        warn_limit: None,
   397→    };
   398→
   399→    vvc::commit(&commit_args)
   400→}
   401→
   402→fn zjjrx_run_chalk(args: jjrx_ChalkArgs) -> i32 {
   403→    let marker = match ChalkMarker::jjrn_parse(&args.marker) {
   404→        Ok(m) => m,
   405→        Err(e) => {
   406→            eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: {}", e);
   407→            return 1;
   408→        }
   409→    };
   410→
   411→    // Try parsing as Coronet first (5 base64 chars), then as Firemark (2 base64 chars)
   412→    let identity = args.identity.strip_prefix('₢').or_else(|| args.identity.strip_prefix('₣')).unwrap_or(&args.identity);
   413→
   414→    let message = if identity.len() == 5 {
   415→        // Coronet - pace-level chalk
   416→        let coronet = match Coronet::jjrf_parse(&args.identity) {
   417→            Ok(c) => c,
   418→            Err(e) => {
   419→                eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: {}", e);
   420→                return 1;
   421→            }
   422→        };
   423→        format_chalk_message(&coronet, marker, &args.description)
   424→    } else if identity.len() == 2 {
   425→        // Firemark - heat-level (discussion only)
   426→        if marker.jjrn_requires_pace() {
   427→            eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: {} marker requires a Coronet (pace identity), not a Firemark", marker.jjrn_as_str());
   428→            return 1;
   429→        }
   430→        let firemark = match Firemark::jjrf_parse(&args.identity) {
   431→            Ok(fm) => fm,
   432→            Err(e) => {
   433→                eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: {}", e);
   434→                return 1;
   435→            }
   436→        };
   437→        format_heat_discussion(&firemark, &args.description)
   438→    } else {
   439→        eprintln!("jjx_chalk: error: identity must be Coronet (5 chars) or Firemark (2 chars), got {} chars", identity.len());
   440→        return 1;
   441→    };
   442→
   443→    let commit_args = vvc::vvcc_CommitArgs {
   444→        prefix: None,
   445→        message: Some(message),
   446→        allow_empty: true,
   447→        no_stage: true,
   448→        size_limit: None,
   449→        warn_limit: None,
   450→    };
   451→
   452→    vvc::commit(&commit_args)
   453→}

   565→fn zjjrx_run_retire(args: zjjrx_RetireArgs) -> i32 {
   566→    use crate::jjrc_core::jjrc_timestamp_date;
   567→    use crate::jjrg_gallops::jjrg_RetireArgs as LibRetireArgs;
   568→    use crate::jjrs_steeplechase::{jjrs_ReinArgs as ReinArgs, jjrs_get_entries as get_entries};
   569→    use std::path::Path;
   570→
   571→    let firemark = match Firemark::jjrf_parse(&args.firemark) {
   572→        Ok(fm) => fm,
   573→        Err(e) => {
   574→            eprintln!("jjx_retire: error: {}", e);
   575→            return 1;
   576→        }
   577→    };
   578→
   579→    // Load gallops
   580→    let gallops = match Gallops::jjrg_load(&args.file) {
   581→        Ok(g) => g,
   582→        Err(e) => {
   583→            eprintln!("jjx_retire: error loading Gallops: {}", e);
   584→            return 1;
   585→        }
   586→    };
   587→
   588→    // Get steeplechase entries (filtered by firemark identity)
   589→    let rein_args = ReinArgs {
   590→        firemark: args.firemark.clone(),
   591→        limit: 1000,
   592→    };
   593→    let steeplechase = match get_entries(&rein_args) {
   594→        Ok(entries) => entries,
   595→        Err(e) => {
   596→            eprintln!("jjx_retire: warning: could not get steeplechase: {}", e);
   597→            Vec::new()
   598→        }
   599→    };
   600→
   601→    // Compute base path from gallops file
   602→    let base_path = args.file.parent()
   603→        .and_then(|p| p.parent())
   604→        .and_then(|p| p.parent())
   605→        .unwrap_or(Path::new("."));
   606→
   607→    // If --execute not specified, output trophy markdown preview
   608→    if !args.execute {
   609→        // Read paddock content
   610→        let firemark_key = firemark.jjrf_display();
   611→        let heat = match gallops.heats.get(&firemark_key) {
   612→            Some(h) => h,
   613→            None => {
   614→                eprintln!("jjx_retire: error: Heat '{}' not found", firemark_key);
   615→                return 1;
   616→            }
   617→        };
   618→        let paddock_path = base_path.join(&heat.paddock_file);
   619→        let paddock_content = match std::fs::read_to_string(&paddock_path) {
   620→            Ok(c) => c,
   621→            Err(e) => {
   622→                eprintln!("jjx_retire: error reading paddock: {}", e);
   623→                return 1;
   624→            }
   625→        };
   626→
   627→        // Build and output trophy preview
   628→        let today = jjrc_timestamp_date();
   629→        match gallops.jjrg_build_trophy_preview(&args.firemark, &paddock_content, &today, &steeplechase) {
   630→            Ok(markdown) => {
   631→                println!("{}", markdown);
   632→                return 0;
   633→            }
   634→            Err(e) => {
   635→                eprintln!("jjx_retire: error: {}", e);
   636→                return 1;
   637→            }
   638→        }
   639→    }
   640→
   641→    // --execute: perform the actual retire operation
   642→
   643→    // Need mutable gallops for execute path
   644→    let mut gallops = gallops;
   645→
   646→    // Acquire lock FIRST
   647→    let lock = match vvc::vvcc_CommitLock::vvcc_acquire() {
   648→        Ok(l) => l,
   649→        Err(e) => {
   650→            eprintln!("jjx_retire: error: {}", e);
   651→            return 1;
   652→        }
   653→    };
   654→
   655→    // Execute retire
   656→    let retire_args = LibRetireArgs {
   657→        firemark: args.firemark.clone(),
   658→        today: jjrc_timestamp_date(),
   659→    };
   660→
   661→    let result = match gallops.jjrg_retire(retire_args, base_path, &steeplechase) {
   662→        Ok(r) => r,
   663→        Err(e) => {
   664→            eprintln!("jjx_retire: error: {}", e);
   665→            return 1;
   666→        }
   667→    };
   668→
   669→    // Save gallops
   670→    if let Err(e) = gallops.jjrg_save(&args.file) {
   671→        eprintln!("jjx_retire: error saving Gallops: {}", e);
   672→        return 1;
   673→    }
   674→
   675→    // Commit using vvcm_commit with explicit file list
   676→    // Files: gallops.json, trophy file (created), paddock file (deleted - git add handles this)
   677→    let gallops_path = args.file.to_string_lossy().to_string();
   678→    let commit_args = vvc::vvcm_CommitArgs {
   679→        files: vec![
   680→            gallops_path,
   681→            result.trophy_path.clone(),
   682→            result.paddock_path.clone(),
   683→        ],
   684→        message: format_heat_message(&firemark, HeatAction::Retire, &result.silks),
   685→        size_limit: 200000,  // 200KB - trophy files can be large
   686→        warn_limit: 100000,
   687→    };
   688→
   689→    match vvc::machine_commit(&lock, &commit_args) {
   690→        Ok(hash) => {
   691→            eprintln!("jjx_retire: committed {}", &hash[..8]);
   692→        }
   693→        Err(e) => {
   694→            eprintln!("jjx_retire: commit warning: {}", e);
   695→        }
   696→    }
   697→
   698→    // Output result
   699→    println!("trophy: {}", result.trophy_path);
   700→    println!("Heat {} retired successfully", result.firemark);
   701→
   702→    0
   703→    // lock released here
   704→}
